figures.caption: Fig. 1. The Cognitive Circuit Breaker architecture. Hidden states are extracted at an optimized intermediate layer (Lopt) and evaluated by a pre-trained linear probe. The resulting internal certainty (Platent) is compared against the final layer’s semantic confidence (Psemantic) to compute the Cognitive Dissonance Delta (∆).
TABLE I FINAL EXPERIMENT SUMMARY (BALANCED CLASSES, F1-MAXIMIZED THRESHOLDS)

Fig. 2. AUROC emergence plots across normalized layer depth for all three evaluated architectures. Curves represent the mean extraction mode, with black circles (◦) denoting the optimal extraction layer (Lopt) where internal certainty peaks for each dataset. These peaks are highly architecture-dependent (e.g., DeepSeek peaks centrally, Gemma peaks late). However, across all models and datasets, the final layers experience “representation collapse,” prioritizing linguistically fluent tokens over factual accuracy.
